dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: How a system is put together, as a diagram — a system-interaction map, a component map, an API contract, an event flow, or an infrastructure picture. Attached to a Product as a whole, or to one of its Solutions.

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.description: The Product this document belongs to

entries.rules: must reference a Solution of the same Product when set
entries.description: The Solution this document describes. Null means it is about the whole Product — a system-interaction map spans several Solutions, an API contract belongs to one

entries.rules: one of: systemInteraction / componentMap / apiContract / eventFlow / infrastructure
entries.description: What sort of picture this is
What the document is called. Regenerating the same name replaces it rather than leaving two

entries.rules: must be valid in its declared format — checked before storing, never after

entries.description: The notation the content is written in
When the document was first stored

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Until the document or its Product is deleted. Deleting a Solution only unlinks its documents — an architecture document usually outlives the Solution it was drawn for, and is often the only record of why the thing was shaped that way.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: The content is valid in its declared format, checked before it is stored and never after. A diagram that does not render is worse than no diagram: it looks like documentation, so the gap stops being visible and nobody writes the real thing. A document about a Solution names a Solution of its own Product.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.

rounds.change: New: Developer Planning needs somewhere to keep architecture — system interactions, component maps, API contracts, event flows, infrastructure — that the AI can draw and the app can validate, show and emit.
